name: Tadalafil Exercise Capacity And Physical Function Search 2026-06-06

search_queries.query: tadalafil exercise capacity healthy subjects randomized trial PubMed
tadalafil exercise performance healthy men randomized trial PubMed
tadalafil exercise muscle damage redox healthy men PubMed
tadalafil Duchenne Becker muscular dystrophy 6-minute walk randomized trial PubMed
tadalafil COPD pulmonary hypertension 6-minute walk randomized trial PubMed
PDE5 sildenafil hypoxia exercise performance systematic review PubMed

inclusion_criteria: Human tadalafil studies measuring exercise capacity, physical performance, functional status, muscle perfusion, body composition, or exercise-linked recovery biomarkers.
Disease-specific tadalafil functional-outcome trials when they define transfer boundaries for PAH, COPD-PH, CAD, or muscular dystrophy.
Non-tadalafil PDE5 evidence only when it addresses exercise performance and is explicitly marked adjacent under the class-transfer framework.
Current safety/interaction sources needed to interpret exertion, blood pressure, nitrate, and cardiovascular boundaries.
exclusion_criteria: ED intercourse-performance studies without exercise, physical-function, body-composition, recovery, or safety-exertion endpoints.
Animal or in vitro-only studies unless paired with a human physical-function signal.
Combination altitude-illness prevention studies where tadalafil monotherapy exercise performance cannot be isolated.
Anecdotal bodybuilding, pump, or forum reports.
dedupe_method: Deduplicated by PMID/DOI and existing source id.

screening_counts.records_identified: Targeted search, not a systematic count

screening_decisions.title: The long-acting phosphodiesterase inhibitor tadalafil does not influence athletes' VO2max, aerobic, and anaerobic thresholds in normoxia.
Effect of tadalafil on anaerobic performance indices in healthy athletes.
The phosphodiesterases type 5 inhibitor tadalafil reduces the activation of the hypothalamus-pituitary-adrenal axis in men during cycle ergometric exercise.
Effects of tadalafil administration on plasma markers of exercise-induced muscle damage, IL6 and antioxidant status capacity.
Effect of Tadalafil Administration on Redox Homeostasis and Polyamine Levels in Healthy Men with High Level of Physical Activity.
Tadalafil improves lean mass and endothelial function in nonobese men with mild ED/LUTS.
Tadalafil alleviates muscle ischemia in patients with Becker muscular dystrophy.
A phase 3 randomized placebo-controlled trial of tadalafil for Duchenne muscular dystrophy.
Tadalafil for veterans with chronic obstructive pulmonary disease-pulmonary hypertension.
The effect of tadalafil on the time to exercise-induced myocardial ischaemia in subjects with coronary artery disease.
Sildenafil does not reliably improve exercise performance in hypoxia: a systematic review.
Tadalafil therapy for pulmonary arterial hypertension.
DailyMed CIALIS prescribing information.
screening_decisions.reason: Existing safety-context source required for exertion, blood-pressure, nitrate, and interaction boundaries.

screening_decisions.title: Time course of the interaction between tadalafil and nitrates.
screening_decisions.reason: Existing safety-context source required for exercise and nitrate hypotension boundaries.

screening_decisions.title: Interaction between tadalafil and alpha-blockers.
screening_decisions.reason: Existing safety-context source required for exertion plus alpha-blocker hypotension boundaries.

screening_decisions.title: Analysis of integrated clinical safety data of tadalafil in patients receiving antihypertensive medications.
screening_decisions.reason: Existing safety-context source required for antihypertensive co-use and blood-pressure-risk framing.

screening_decisions.title: The type 5 phosphodiesterase inhibitor tadalafil influences salivary cortisol, testosterone, and DHEAS responses to maximal exercise in healthy men.
screening_decisions.reason: Relevant endocrine exercise signal but superseded for this baseline by the later 2012 HPA-axis exercise study.

screening_decisions.title: Tadalafil and acetazolamide versus acetazolamide for the prevention of severe high-altitude illness.
screening_decisions.reason: Combination altitude-illness prevention context; tadalafil monotherapy exercise-performance effect cannot be isolated.

screening_decisions.title: Tadalafil improved erectile function at twenty-four and thirty-six hours after dosing in men with erectile dysfunction.
screening_decisions.reason: ED intercourse endpoint, not exercise or physical-function outcome.

screening_summary: Targeted baseline search identified direct tadalafil studies in healthy athletes/fit men, ED/LUTS body composition, muscular dystrophy, COPD-PH, CAD exertion safety, and PAH functional capacity, plus one sildenafil hypoxia systematic review as adjacent PDE5 class evidence. The direct healthy-performance evidence is small and mostly null; disease-specific evidence was retained to define transfer boundaries.
limitations: Targeted baseline search rather than systematic review.
No full-text quantitative synthesis of athlete or recovery studies in this pass.
Sildenafil hypoxia evidence is adjacent and not tadalafil-specific.
